announcements.headline: Updates
announcements.body: ICICI Bank Limited has informed the Exchange regarding ':  Receipt of Observation Letters communicating  No Objection  from the National Stock Exchange of India Limited and BSE Limited in relation to the proposed delisting of equity shares of ICICI Securities Limited ( ICICI Securities ) pursuant to a scheme of arrangement'.

announcements.headline: Updates
announcements.body: ICICI Bank Limited has informed the Exchange regarding 'Outcome of Board Meeting - Proposed delisting of equity shares of ICICI Securities pursuant to a scheme of arrangement '.

announcements.headline: Updates
announcements.body: ICICI Bank Limited has informed the Exchange regarding 'This is further to our letter dated March 13, 2020, we wish to inform you that Yes Bank Limited has credited 1,000,000,000 equity shares at a price of ₹ 10.0 per share (face value ₹ 2.0 per share at a share premium of ₹ 8.0 per share) on March 18, 2020 into the client account of ICICI Bank Limited aggregating to 7.97% of the post issue Share Capital of Yes Bank Limited, under the Scheme of Reconstruction of Yes Bank Limited under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, notified by the Government of India. This follows the allotment on March 14, 2020.'.

announcements.headline: News Verification
announcements.body: The Exchange has sought clarification from ICICI Bank Limited with respect to recent news item captioned "ICICI Bank moves NCLT against Punj Lloyd under Insolvency Code". The Exchange, in order to verify the accuracy or otherwise of the information reported in the media and to inform the market place so that the interest of the investors is safeguarded, had written to the company. ICICI Bank Limited has submitted their response.

announcements.headline: Updates
announcements.body: ICICI Bank Limited has informed the Exchange regarding 'With reference to our earlier announcement dated June 14, 2018 regarding news item appearing in the ��www.livemint.com��captioned "ICICI Bank moves NCLT against Punj Lloyd under Insolvency Code", we would like to inform you that the above mentioned announcement was in response to the letter/emails received from both the exchanges dated June 14, 2018 seeking clarification on the news item.We request you to please take the above on record.'.

announcements.headline: Updates
announcements.body: ICICI Bank Limited has informed the Exchange regarding 'ICICI Bank Limited filed an insolvency petition under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 against Punj Lloyd Limited before the NCLT Principal Bench, New Delhi on 28 May 2018. On June 14, 2018, the NCLT issued notice to Punj Lloyd Limited to file their reply within 2 weeks. The petition has been filed in the ordinary course of business against a defaulting borrower and has been determined to not be material to ICICI Bank and does not warrant disclosure to the stock exchanges.'.

announcements.headline: News Verification
announcements.body: The Exchange has sought clarification from ICICI Bank Limited with respect to recent news item captioned "ICICI Bank moves NCLT against Punj Lloyd under Insolvency Code". In this regard, Exchange have advised the Company to provide clarification/confirmation on the news item in detail including the following: a) Whether you/company are aware of any information that has not been announced to the Exchanges . The response from the company is awaited.

announcements.headline: Updates
announcements.body: ICICI Bank Limited has informed the Exchange regarding 'Pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations & Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 read with SEBI circular no. CIR/CFD/CMD/4/2015 dated September 9, 2015, we wish to inform you that the Board at its Meeting held on March 12, 2020 accorded approval for an equity investment of up to Rs. 10.00 billion in equity shares of Yes Bank Limited, comprising up to 1.00 billion equity shares at a price of Rs. 10.0 per share, under the proposed Scheme of Reconstruction of Yes Bank Limited under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, subject to regulatory and government approval. This investment is likely to result in ICICI Bank Limited holding in excess of 5.0% shareholding in Yes Bank Limited, with the final shareholding to be determined based on the final Scheme of Reconstruction and share issuance thereunder. Attached is the disclosure with respect to the Bank s proposed investment in equity shares of Yes Bank Limited.'.

announcements.headline: Updates
announcements.body: ICICI Bank Limited has informed the Exchange regarding 'Company Update'.- We have been informed by ICICI Lombard General Insurance Company Limited (ICICI Lombard) that their Board of Directors at the Meeting held on September 8, 2021 had allotted equity shares of ICICI Lombard to the eligible shareholders of Bharti AXA General Insurance Company Limited ( Bharti AXA ) as per their proportionate shareholding on the Record Date i.e. August 21, 2020 pursuant to the Scheme of Arrangement amongst Bharti AXA and ICICI Lombard and their respective shareholders and creditors. ICICI Lombard has made disclosure to the stock exchanges vide their letter Ref no: MUM/SEC/143-09/2022 on September 8, 2021. Accordingly, with the Bank s shareholding reducing from 51.86% to 48.08%, ICICI Lombard has ceased to be a subsidiary of the Bank.

announcements.headline: Updates
announcements.body: ICICI Bank Limited has informed the Exchange regarding 'This is further to our letter dated June 6, 2018 filed earlier in the day with the subject ��Company Update��.With respect to clarifications sought under paras (a), (b) and (c) of email dated 6th June 2018 received from NSE and the email dated 6th June, 2018 received from BSE. We wish to clarify as under: The article already carries a statement from the Bank that it is yet to receive a copy of the NCLT order. This continues to be the position at the point of sending you this clarification.It is further clarified that the matter does not require disclosure as prescribed under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, read with the Materiality Policy of the Bank.'.

announcements.headline: News Clarification
announcements.body: The Exchange had sought clarification from the Company with respect to news item captioned- "NCLT directs ICICI Bank to reverse Rs48 crore in Ruchi Soyas current account". The response from the Company is attached.

announcements.headline: Updates
announcements.body: ICICI Bank Limited has informed the Exchange regarding 'We refer to the emails dated June 6, 2018 received from the stock exchanges seeking clarification on the media report titled ��NCLT directs ICICI Bank to reverse Rs 48 crore in Ruchi Soya��s current account��. We would like to reiterate that as already clarified in the news article, the Bank is yet to receive a copy of the order and would decide the future course of legal action upon receipt of certified copy of the order. We request you to please take this submission on record. There is no matter at this point of time which requires disclosure as per Regulation 30 of the Listing Regulations. '.

announcements.headline: News Clarification
announcements.body: The Exchange has sought clarification from the Company with respect to news item captioned- "NCLT directs ICICI Bank to reverse Rs48 crore in Ruchi Soyas current account" In this regard, Exchange has advised the Company to provide clarification/confirmation on the news item in detail including the following: a) Whether such event/negotiations/article stated in published news were taking place? If so, you are advised to provide the said information along with the sequence of events in chronological order and the material impact of this article on the Company. b) Whether company is aware of any information that has not been announced to the Exchanges under regulation 30 of Listing Regulations. The response from the Company is awaited.

announcements.headline: Updates
announcements.body: ICICI Bank Limited has informed the Exchange regarding 'The Board of the Bank approved the appointment of Mr. Sandeep Batra as an Executive Director, subject to RBI approval for a period of five years on May 6, 2019. The effective date of the appointment was to be from the date of RBI approval for the proposal made. Disclosure was made on May 6, 2019 accordingly. SEBI passed an order on September 12, 2019 inter-alia levying a penalty of   Rs. 2 lakh on  Mr. Sandeep Batra, in his capacity as the Compliance Officer of the Bank in 2010. The order was in respect of a matter wherein ICICI Bank Limited had made a disclosure after its Board meeting held on May 18, 2010 providing an in-principle approval for the amalgamation of The Bank of Rajasthan Ltd. (eBoR) with the Bank. The Bank had entered into an agreement earlier on the same day with certain shareholders of eBoR. The disclosure made by the Bank after the Board meeting was construed as delayed disclosure by SEBI while issuing the order. The Bank had made disclosure to the stock exchanges with regard to the SEBI order on September 13, 2019.Settlement proceedings under SEBI (Settlement Proceedings) Regulations, 2018 have been initiated in this regard by Mr. Batra.The Bank has received a communication from RBI not acceding to the request for appointment of Mr. Batra at present and to resubmit the proposal for approval after one year from the conclusion of settlement proceedings.Please take the above information pursuant to the requirements of Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 on record.'.
